name: airo-tulip

metadata.name: airo-tulip
metadata.summary: Python driver for the KELO Robile platform based on KELO tulip.

documentation:
# AIRO Tulip

The `airo-tulip` package is a Python port of the KELO Tulip software with some additional functionality.
You can use it to control the KELO Robile drives of a mobile platform and read out relevant sensor data from it.
The structure of the code base is different from that of the original C++ implementation provided by the manufacturer.

In this README, we over the structure of the `airo-tulip` package and discuss some design choices that were made during the implementation.

The `docs` folder contains additional documentation files (in no particular order):

- `kelo_setup.md` on how to set up the KELO hardware and software, a prerequisite to using `airo-tulip`
- `rerun.md` on how to use Rerun running on the remote KELO CPU brick
- `virtual_display.md` on how to enable a virtual display for use in a VNC connection without needing to connect a display to the KELO CPU brick
- `external_devices.md` on how to connect to other devices mounted on the KELO, with the KELO acting as the router
- `how_it_works.md` on how the code base is structured and why

Even though you don't need to understand or even read *all* of these to use `airo-tulip`,
we do recommend it to get a holistic overview of how the platform works and
why `airo-tulip` is implemented the way it is.

## How to use this package

There are two layers to the `airo-tulip` package: the `hardware` module that interfaces with the KELO and other attached hardware,
and the `api` module that allows (optionally remote) users of the library to interface with this `hardware` over a TCP connection.
The code in the `hardware` module is supposed to be run on the KELO itself, while the code in the `api` module (more specifically in `api.client`)
can be run from any device that can access the KELO over Ethernet or Wi-Fi. While it possible to use `airo-tulip` without
`api`, by using `hardware.platform_driver` directly, this is not recommended, and we only document the usage through the `api` module.

**Note:** when interfacing with the server from a client on a remote machine, make sure that the `airo-tulip` versions
match on client and server, or else you may observe unexpected behaviour or crashes.

Using this package implies some hardware and software set-up on the KELO mobile platform itself, which is documented
in [`docs/kelo_setup.md`](docs/kelo_setup.md). If you simply use this library for robotics experiments, this set-up
will most likely have been performed for you. Still, please read that file thoroughly before continuing.
If you are the first to set up your custom KELO mobile platform, it is also recommended to read that file to get
up and running quickly.

### Installation

#### From PyPI

This package is available [on PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/airo-tulip/) and can be installed with one command:

```shell
pip install airo-tulip
```

Note that the package requires at least Python 3.9.

For example, using [pyenv](https://github.com/pyenv) to set the local Python version to 3.9:

```shell
pyenv install 3.9 && pyenv local 3.9
python3 -m venv env
source env/bin/activate
pip install airo-tulip
```

Or, using conda:

```shell
conda create -n airo-tulip-env python=3.9
conda activate airo-tulip-env
pip install airo-tulip
```

#### From GitHub

If you wish to install a development version, clone the repository with `git` and use `pip` to install the `airo-tulip/` package
in editable mode.
Note that the `main` branch is the active development branch: you may want to check out a certain commit associated with
a version tag.

Using pyenv:

```shell
git clone https://github.com/airo-ugent/airo_kelo
cd airo_kelo
pyenv install 3.9 && pyenv local 3.9
python3 -m venv env
source env/bin/activate
pip install -e airo-tulip/
```

Or using conda:

```shell
git clone https://github.com/airo-ugent/airo_kelo
cd airo_kelo
conda create -n airo-tulip-env python=3.9
conda activate airo-tulip-env
pip install -e airo-tulip/
```

### Running `airo-tulip` on the KELO

`airo_tulip.api.server` provides a class `TulipServer` which initializes the KELO platform and accepts an incoming
connection from a `airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile`. To accept incoming connections from any device on the network,
listen on the IPv4 address `0.0.0.0`. The `RobotConfiguration` that must be supplied, is based on how the KELO bricks
are mounted. This information is received from KELO robotics together with your platform and is specific to your use case.
The EtherCAT device is also specific to your platform set-up.

To run the server, start it from a Python script:

```python
from airo_tulip.api.server import TulipServer, RobotConfiguration
from airo_tulip.hardware.structs import WheelConfig

def create_wheel_configs():
    wheel_configs = []

    wc0 = WheelConfig(
        ethercat_number=3,
        x=0.233,
        y=0.1165,
        a=1.57
    )
    wheel_configs.append(wc0)

    wc1 = WheelConfig(
        ethercat_number=5,
        x=0.233,
        y=-0.1165,
        a=1.57
    )
    wheel_configs.append(wc1)

    wc2 = WheelConfig(
        ethercat_number=7,
        x=-0.233,
        y=-0.1165,
        a=-1.57
    )
    wheel_configs.append(wc2)

    wc3 = WheelConfig(
        ethercat_number=9,
        x=-0.233,
        y=0.1165,
        a=1.57
    )
    wheel_configs.append(wc3)

    return wheel_configs

# These values are specific to your platform!
device = "eno1"
wheel_configs = create_wheel_configs()

server = TulipServer(RobotConfiguration(device, wheel_configs), "0.0.0.0")
server.run()
```

### Connecting to the `airo-tulip` server

Once you have started the server on the KELO, you can connect to it with an `api.client.KELORobile` instance:

```python
from airo_tulip.api.client import KELORobile

kelo_ip = "10.10.129.21"
client = KELORobile(kelo_ip)
```

You can then send commands to the KELO platform by calling the methods on the `client` object, e.g.,

```python
client.set_platform_velocity_target(0.5, 0.0, 0.0, timeout=1.0)
```

to drive approximately 0.5 meters, at 0.5 meters per second, along the platform's +X axis.

### Mounted devices

Without mounting external devices on the KELO, you can pretty much only drive around (which is cool, but not very useful).
Refer to [`docs/external_devices.md`](docs/external_devices.md) for information on how to set up and access external devices
such as a UR cobot with a Robotiq gripper.

## Structure

For more information on how this code base is structured, and why, please refer to [`docs/how_it_works.md`](docs/how_it_works.md).

modules.name: airo_tulip.api.messages
documentation: These messages are used to communicate between client (KELORobile) and server (TulipServer).

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.RequestMessage
documentation: Base class for all request messages.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.ResponseMessage
documentation: Base class for all response messages.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.HandshakeMessage
documentation: A handshake message is used to establish a connection between client and server.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.HandshakeResponse
documentation: A handshake reply is used to confirm a connection between client and server.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.AreDrivesAlignedMessage
documentation: A message to check if the drives are aligned.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.SetPlatformVelocityTargetMessage
documentation: A message to set the platform velocity target.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.SetDriverTypeMessage
documentation: A message to set the driver type (velocity mode or compliant mode).

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.StopServerMessage
documentation: A message to stop the server.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.GetVelocityMessage
documentation: A message to get the velocity of the robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.GetOdometryMessage
documentation: A message to get the odometry of the robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.ResetOdometryMessage
documentation: A message to reset the odometry of the robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.OdometryResponse
documentation: A response message containing the odometry of the robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.VelocityResponse
documentation: A response message containing the velocity of the robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.AreDrivesAlignedResponse
documentation: A response message containing the alignment status of the drives.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.ErrorResponse
documentation: A response message containing an error message.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.messages.OkResponse
documentation: A response message indicating that the request was successful.

modules.name: airo_tulip.api.client
documentation: The KELORobile client is a client that interfaces with the TulipServer (see server.py).

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobileError
documentation: Error raised when an error occurs in the KELORobile client.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the client and connect to the server.

Args:
    robot_ip: The IP address of the robot. Use 0.0.0.0 for access from the local network.
    robot_port: The port on which to run this server (default: 49789).

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile._handshake
documentation:
Perform a handshake with the server to ensure the connection is established. If the server returns
a different UUID, the connection is not properly, or if nothing is returned, the server is not running.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.set_platform_velocity_target
documentation:
Set the x, y and angular velocity of the complete mobile platform.

Args:
    vel_x: Linear velocity of platform in x (forward) direction in m/s.
    vel_y: Linear velocity of platform in y (left) direction in m/s.
    vel_a: Linear velocity of platform in angular direction in rad/s.
    timeout: Duration in seconds after which the movement is automatically stopped (default 1.0).

Returns:
    A ResponseMessage object indicating the response status of the request.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.align_drives
documentation:
Align the drives for the given velocity values, such that they are oriented correctly. Does not send forward velocities.

Args:
    vel_x: Linear velocity of platform in x (forward) direction in m/s.
    vel_y: Linear velocity of platform in y (left) direction in m/s.
    vel_a: Linear velocity of platform in angular direction in rad/s.
    timeout: Duration in seconds after which the movement is automatically stopped (default 1.0).

Returns:
    A ResponseMessage object indicating the response status of the request.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.are_drives_aligned
documentation:
Check whether the drives are aligned with the last sent velocity command orientation.

Returns:
    A boolean indicating the alignment.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.set_driver_type
documentation:
Set the mode of the platform driver.

Args:
    driver_type: Type to which the driver should be set.

Returns:
    A ResponseMessage object indicating the response status of the request.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.stop_server
documentation:
Stops the remote server.

Returns:
    A ResponseMessage object indicating the response status of the request.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.get_odometry
documentation: Get the robot platform's odometry.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.reset_odometry
documentation: Reset the platform's odometry to 0.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.get_velocity
documentation: Get the robot platform's velocity.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile._transceive_message
documentation: Send a request message to the server and return the response message. Raises a RuntimeError on timeouts.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.client.KELORobile.close
documentation: Close the connection to the server.

The KELORobile is a client that interfaces with the TulipServer (see server.py).

Methods are translated into network calls (essentially performing RPC). All the methods
can raise a KELORobileError in case of an error.

modules.name: airo_tulip.api.server
documentation: The TulipServer accepts incoming connections over TCP to send commands to the mobile robot.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.server.RobotConfiguration
documentation:
The mobile robot configuration requires two parameters: an EtherCAT device string and a list of wheel configurations.

This configuration is required to properly set up the platform and should be passed to the TulipServer's constructor.

classes.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the server.

Args:
    robot_configuration: The robot configuration.
    robot_ip: The IP address of the robot. Use 0.0.0.0 for access from the local network.
    robot_port: The port on which to run this server (default: 49789).
    loop_frequency: The frequency (Hz) with which EtherCAT messages are received and sent.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._request_loop
documentation: The request loop listens for incoming requests and handles them.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._ethercat_loop
documentation: The EtherCAT loop runs at a fixed frequency and steps the platform.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer.run
documentation: Run the server. Starts threads that listen for requests and run the EtherCAT loop.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_request
documentation:
Handle a request message and return a response message.

Args:
    request: The request message.

Returns:
    The response.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_set_platform_velocity_target_request
documentation:
Handle a set platform velocity target request.

Args:
    request: The request message.

Returns:
    A response message.

Raises:
    An ErrorResponse if the safety limits are exceeded.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_are_drives_aligned_request
documentation: Handle a request to check if the drives are aligned.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_reset_odometry_request
documentation: Handle a request to reset the odometry.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_set_driver_type_request
documentation: Handle a request to set the driver type (velocity or compliant mode).

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_stop_server_request
documentation: Handle a request to stop the server.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_get_odometry_request
documentation: Handle a request to get the odometry.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_get_velocity_request
documentation: Handle a request to get the velocity.

methods.name: airo_tulip.api.server.TulipServer._handle_handshake_request
documentation: Handle a handshake request.

The TulipServer accepts incoming connections over TCP to send commands to the mobile
robot (Robile) platform.

The TCP connections could come from the same machine, if you are running application code directly
on the KELO CPU Brick, or over the network, if you are running application code on some remote device
(e.g., your laptop, workstation, or a NUC mounted on the Robile platform.
In any case, application code that wishes to interface with the Robile platform needs
to communicate with the TulipServer over a TCP socket, connecting with 0MQ. We use the REQ/REP
message pattern (https://learning-0mq-with-pyzmq.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pyzmq/patterns/client_server.html).

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.robile_platform
documentation: The RobilePlatform drives the robot through EtherCAT.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.robile_platform.RobilePlatform

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.robile_platform.RobilePlatform.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the RobilePlatform.

Args:
    device: The EtherCAT device name.
    wheel_configs: A list of wheel configurations specific to your platform.
    enable_rerun: Enable logging of monitor values to Rerun. Disabled by default.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.robile_platform.RobilePlatform.init_ethercat
documentation:
Initializes the EtherCAT interface and all connected slaves into an operational state.
Returns `True` if initialisation is successful, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.robile_platform.RobilePlatform.step
documentation: Main processing loop of the EtherCAT master, must be called frequently.
The RobilePlatform drives the robot through EtherCAT.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.controller
documentation: Controller base class with common functionality for all controllers.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.controller.Controller

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.controller.Controller.__init__
documentation:
Initialise.

Args:
    wheel_configs: The configurations for each drive.
Controller base class with common functionality for all controllers.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.constants
documentation:
Constants taken directly from KELO:
https://github.com/kelo-robotics/kelo_tulip/blob/1a8db0626b3d399b62b65b31c004e7b1831756d7/src/PlatformDriver.cpp

variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.constants.MAX_V_LIN_ACC
documentation: per millisecond, same value for deceleration

variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.constants.MAX_ANGLE_ACC
documentation: at vlin=0, per msec, same value for deceleration

variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.constants.MAX_V_A_ACC
documentation: per millisecond, same value for deceleration

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.logging.monitor_rerun
documentation:
Rerun monitor logger for logging all values to rerun.

Note: This currently causes a memory leak, and it is recommended to not use this until
https://github.com/airo-ugent/airo-tulip/issues/25 is resolved.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.logging.monitor_rerun.RerunMonitorLogger.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the Rerun monitor logger.

Args:
    rerun_application_id: Application ID for rerun.
    rerun_max_memory_gb: Maximum amount of memory rerun is allowed to consume.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.logging.monitor_rerun.RerunMonitorLogger.step
documentation: Log all values to rerun.

functions.name: airo_tulip.hardware.util.clip
documentation: Clip a value between a maximum and a minimum value.

functions.name: airo_tulip.hardware.util.clip_angle
documentation: Clip an angle between -pi and pi.

functions.name: airo_tulip.hardware.util.get_shortest_angle
documentation: Get the shortest angle between two angles.

functions.name: airo_tulip.hardware.util.sign
documentation: Get the sign of a number. For 0, returns 1.0

variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.Attitude2DType
documentation: A (3,) np array representing a pose in 2D space with Cartesian coordinates, with `a` the angle measured from the X-axis.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.PlatformLimits
documentation: Configuration of the velocity and acceleration limits of complete mobile platform.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelConfig
documentation: Class for storing the details and geometry of each drive as a part of the complete mobile platform.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.pivot_position
documentation: pivot location relative to vehicle centre

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.pivot_offset
documentation: pivot offset relative to vehicle direction of travel

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.relative_position_l
documentation: location of left wheel relative to pivot

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.relative_position_r
documentation: location of right wheel relative to pivot

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.linear_to_angular_velocity
documentation: scaling m/s to rad/s

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.angular_to_linear_velocity
documentation: scaling rad/s to m/s

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.max_linear_velocity
documentation: maximum velocity of wheel

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.max_pivot_velocity
documentation: maximum pivot error of smart wheel used for error correction

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.pivot_kp
documentation: proportional gain for pivot position controller

class_variables.name: airo_tulip.hardware.structs.WheelParamVelocity.wheel_diameter
documentation: wheel diameter
Class for storing the details and geometry of each drive as a part of the complete mobile platform.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client
documentation: Client to interface with peripherals connected to the Teensy microcontroller.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.__init__
documentation: Initialize the client and connect to the server.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient._transceive
documentation: Send a command to the server and return the response.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.ping
documentation: Check if the server is reachable.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.get_flow
documentation: Get the flow sensor readings.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.set_leds_idle
documentation: Set the LEDs to idle mode.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.set_leds_active
documentation: Set the LEDs to active mode, depending on the current angle and velocity.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.peripheral_client.PeripheralClient.set_leds_error
documentation: Set the LEDs to error mode.
Client to interface with peripherals connected to the Teensy microcontroller.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller
documentation: Control the Robile platform with velocity commands.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the controller.

Args:
    wheel_configs: The configurations for each drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.get_pivot_angle
documentation:
Compute the pivot angle, clipped between -pi and pi, for the current pivot rotation.

Args:
    wheel_param: Drive parameters.
    pivot_encoder_value: Drive pivot encoder value (current pivot angle).

Returns:
    The pivot angle.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.get_pivot_position
documentation:
Compute the vector that points from the centre of the drive to the current wheel pivot.

Args:
    wheel_param: Drive parameters.
    pivot_encoder_value: Drive pivot encoder value (current pivot angle).

Returns:
    The position of the pivot.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.wheel_positions_relative_to_platform_centre
documentation:
For a given drive's parameters and pivot angle, compute the left and right wheel positions with respect
to the platform centre.

Args:
    wheel_param: Drive parameters.
    pivot_encoder_value: Drive pivot encoder value (current pivot angle).

Returns:
    The position of the left and right wheel w.r.t. the platform centre.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.velocity_at_position
documentation:
Given a target velocity vector at the origin (0, 0), compute the target velocity vector at a different position.

Args:
    target_velocity: Target velocity (x, y, angular) at the origin.
    position: The position to compute the target velocity for.

Returns:
    Target velocity at the given position.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.set_platform_velocity_target
documentation:
Set the target velocity of the platform.

Args:
    vel_x: The linear velocity (m/s) along the x axis.
    vel_y: The linear velocity (m/s) along the y axis.
    vel_a: The angular velocity (rad/s) around the center of the platform.
    only_align_drives: If set, does not more the platform but simply aligns the drives.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.set_platform_max_velocity
documentation:
Set the maximum velocity that the platform is allowed to drive at.

Args:
    max_vel_linear: The maximum linear velocity (m/s).
    max_vel_angular: The maximum angular velocity (m/s).

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.set_platform_max_acceleration
documentation: Set the maximum allowed platform acceleration.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.set_platform_max_deceleration
documentation: Set the maximum allowed platform deceleration.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.calculate_platform_ramped_velocities
documentation: Calculate (and store) the ramped velocities for the platform based on the (stored) target velocities.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController._compute_pivot_error
documentation:
Compute the raw pivot error for a given drive.

Args:
    drive_index: Index of the drive.
    raw_pivot_angle: Encoder pivot value for this drive.

Returns:
    Error of the drive pivot (radians) w.r.t. target angle of the platform.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.are_drives_aligned
documentation:
Returns true when all drives are approximately aligned to drive in the correct direction.

Args:
    encoder_pivots: Encoder pivot values for all drives.
    max_pivot_error: If ALL pivot errors are smaller than this angle (radians), the drives are considered aligned.

Returns:
    True when all drives are approximately aligned to drive in the correct direction.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.controllers.velocity_platform_controller.VelocityPlatformController.calculate_wheel_target_velocity
documentation:
Calculate the wheel velocity setpoints based on the set target velocity.

Args:
    drive_index: Index of the drive.
    raw_pivot_angle: Encoder pivot value for this drive.

Returns:
    The target velocities for the right and left wheel, respectively.
Control the Robile platform with velocity commands.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver
documentation: Platform driver for the airo-tulip platform.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriverType
documentation: Platform driver type (velocity or compliant modes).

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriverState
documentation: Platform driver state.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the platform driver.

Args:
    master: The EtherCAT master.
    wheel_configs: The configurations for each drive.
    controller_type: The type of controller to use (velocity or compliant mode).
    peripheral_client: The peripheral client.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver.set_platform_velocity_target
documentation:
Set the platform's velocity target.

This sets a target velocity for the platform, which it will attempt to achieve. This only works if the driver
is set to velocity mode. An internal check is done on the magnitude of the velocity to ensure safety.
ONLY OVERRIDE THESE CHECKS IF YOU KNOW WHAT YOU ARE DOING.

Args:
    vel_x: Velocity along X axis.
    vel_y: Velocity along Y axis.
    vel_a: Angular velocity.
    timeout: The platform will stop after this many seconds.
    only_align_drives: If true, the platform will only align the wheels in the correct orientation without driving into that directino.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver.are_drives_aligned
documentation: Check if the drives are aligned with the last provided velocity command.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver.set_driver_type
documentation: Set the driver type (velocity control or compliant control).

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver.step
documentation: Perform a single step of the platform driver.

documentation: Step the platform driver in ACTIVE state.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver._step_error
documentation: Step the platform driver in ERROR state.

documentation: Stop the platform.

documentation: Control the platform.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_driver.PlatformDriver._control_velocity_torque
documentation: Control the torque of a wheel.

modules.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor
documentation: This module contains the PlatformMonitor class, which is responsible for monitoring the robot platform's state.

functions.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor._norm_angle
documentation: Normalize an angle to be between -PI and PI radians.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the pose estimator.

Args:
    num_drives: The number of drives.
    wheel_configs: The configurations for each drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator.reset
documentation: Reset the pose estimator odometry values.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator._estimate_velocity
documentation:
Estimate, from the encoder values, the robot platform's linear and angular velocity.

Args:
    dt: Seconds since last iteration.
    encoder_values: Values for the encoders for every drive (x, y, a), accumulated over time.
    cur_pivots: Current pivot values.

Returns:
    vx, vy, va.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator._estimate_pose
documentation:
Estimate the robot platform's pose, based on its estimated velocity and previous estimations.

Args:
    dt: Seconds since last iteration.
    estimated_velocity: The most recent velocity estimation (see _estimate_velocity).

Returns:
    The estimated pose (x, y, a) of the platform.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimator.get_odometry
documentation:
Get the robot platform's odometry.

Args:
    dt: Seconds since last iteration.
    encoder_values: Values for the encoders for every drive (x, y, a), accumulated over time.
    cur_pivots: The current pivot values.

Returns:
    The pose (x, y, a) of the platform and the velocity of the platform.
Estimate the robot platform's pose and velocity based on encoder values and pivot values.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimatorFused

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimatorFused.__init__
documentation: Initialise the fused pose estimator.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimatorFused.transition_function
documentation: Transition function for the Kalman filter.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimatorFused.observation_function
documentation: Observation function for the Kalman filter.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformPoseEstimatorFused.get_pose
documentation:
Update the robot platform's estimated pose by fusing various sensor data using a Kalman filter.

Args:
    TODO

Returns:
    The pose (x, y, a) of the platform.
Estimate the robot platform's pose and velocity based on encoder values, pivot values, and flow sensor data.

classes.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the platform monitor.

Args:
    master: The EtherCAT master.
    wheel_configs: The configurations for each drive.
    peripheral_client: The peripheral client, if available. If not available, sensor readings are affected!

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor._update_encoders
documentation: Update the encoder values for the robot platform.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.step
documentation: Update the robot platform's state.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_estimated_robot_pose
documentation: Get the robot platform's estimated pose based on fused estimator.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_estimated_velocity
documentation: Get the robot platform's estimated velocity based on odometry.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_status1
documentation: Returns the status1 register value for a specific drive, see `ethercat.py`.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_status2
documentation: Returns the status2 register value for a specific drive, see `ethercat.py`.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_encoder
documentation: Returns a list of the encoder value for wheel1, wheel2 and pivot for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_velocity
documentation: Returns a list of the velocity value for wheel1, wheel2 and pivot encoders for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_current
documentation: Returns a list of the direct current for wheel1 and wheel2 for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_voltage
documentation: Returns a list of the pwm voltage for wheel1 and wheel2 for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_temperature
documentation: Returns a list of the temperature for wheel1, wheel2 and IMU for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_voltage_bus
documentation: Returns the bus voltage for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_voltage_bus_max
documentation: Returns the maximal bus voltage of all drives.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_acceleration
documentation: Returns a list of the x, y and z acceleration values for IMU of a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_gyro
documentation: Returns a list of the x, y and z gyro values for IMU of a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_pressure
documentation: Returns the pressure for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_current_in
documentation: Returns the input current for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_current_in_total
documentation: Returns the total input current for all drives.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_power
documentation: Returns the power for a specific drive.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_power_total
documentation: Returns the total power for all drives.

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_flow
documentation: Returns the total accumulated flow ticks for x and y

methods.name: airo_tulip.hardware.platform_monitor.PlatformMonitor.get_orientation
documentation: Returns the orientation measured by the BNO055
Monitor the robot platform's state from EtherCAT messages.
